description:
# Why

bl-f37b ships `bl show --resolve-remote` against a balls-owned cache that
fetches `delivered_repo` with `--filter=blob:none` (commit metadata only, no
trees/blobs). Partial-clone support is universal on modern GitHub/GitLab
but not on older self-hosted deployments — older Bitbucket Server (pre-7.x,
roughly), gitea ahead of partial-clone, plain `git daemon`, etc. — where
the fetch errors with `filter "blob:none" not supported`.

In the current code path that is a hard failure on the first call:
`delivery_remote::ensure_cache` tears the half-built scaffold down,
warns once, and returns `None`. The operator sees `delivered_in_resolved:
null` plus a generic "could not fetch" warning, with no hint that it is
the *filter* the server rejected — and no path forward short of dropping
`--resolve-remote` (which defeats the point).

This is precisely the case we said soft-fail covered, and it does for the
"genuinely unreachable URL" tier. It does not cover "server is reachable
but does not speak partial clone," which is functionally different.

# Scope

In `delivery_remote::ensure_cache`, try `--filter=blob:none` first; on
non-zero exit, retry once with no filter before treating the fetch as
failed. The unfiltered retry pays full blobs but still terminates — the
warm-cache + soft-fail invariants of the bl-f37b design are preserved.

Preferred shape (see "Open" below): detect the specific "filter ... not
supported" error on stderr and only fall back in that case — server-
genuinely-unreachable should not pay a second round-trip. String-matching
git stderr is fragile, but the alternative (always-retry) doubles the
network cost on every legitimately unreachable URL.

Test coverage: a tempdir-backed "remote" with `uploadpack.allowFilter =
false` plus `transfer.advertiseObjectInfo = false` (or whatever the
minimal config is to refuse partial clone) exercises the fallback locally
without a real Bitbucket Server.

# Open

- **Detect via stderr vs always-retry.** stderr capture means using
  `.output()` instead of `.status()` in the fetch path; the existing
  `is_ok_and(|s| s.success())` shape loses stderr. Refactor needed but
  small.
- **What does the unsupporting-server error actually look like?** Worth
  reproducing against an older bitbucket / `git daemon` once, so the
  match string is grounded in a real failure rather than a guess.
- **One-shot capability cache?** Once we know a URL does not support
  filter, the next `ensure_cache` call against it could skip straight
  to unfiltered. Probably premature — the warm cache absorbs most
  subsequent calls anyway — but worth a note.

# Origin

bl-f37b review pass — flagged as a hard failure for users on older
Bitbucket Server / gitea / `git daemon` deployments. Filed because the
read-side resolution is unusable for them otherwise, and the fix is
small enough to land as a follow-up.
title: delivery_remote: fall back to unfiltered fetch when server rejects --filter=blob:none
